message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: High-stakes subprocess sandbox settings (permission_mode/allowed_tools/setting_sources) are never asserted
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py 53-159
DESCRIPTION: _high_stakes_complete builds ClaudeAgentOptions with permission_mode='dontAsk', setting_sources=[], allowed_tools=[] (seam.py:382-389) — the sandbox that stops the subscription subprocess from executing arbitrary tools or reading project/user settings. No drain test reads any of these option fields. VERIFIED non-discriminating: changing permission_mode to 'bypassPermissions' and allowed_tools to ['Bash'] (which would let the high-stakes Claude subprocess run arbitrary shell commands on the production node) passes all 12 drain tests. A regression weakening the sandbox would be invisible to CI.
SUGGESTED FIX: Capture the constructed ClaudeAgentOptions in the fixture and assert permission_mode == 'dontAsk', allowed_tools == [], and setting_sources == [] (and system_prompt/model are threaded through). Pair with the env assertion above so the whole ADR-0003 hardening of the subprocess is locked down by tests.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate this claim by reading the actual code and tests.

content.content:
1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	@pytest.fixture
59	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
60	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream."""
61	    scripted: list = []
62	
63	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
64	        for msg in scripted:
65	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
66	                raise msg
67	            yield msg
68	
69	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
70	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
71	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
72	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
73	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
74	    mod.query = query
75	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
76	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
77	    return scripted
78	
79	
80	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
81	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
82	
83	
84	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
85	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
86	    assert _run() == "pong"
87	
88	
89	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
90	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
91	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
92	        _run()
93	
94	
95	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
96	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
97	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
98	        _run()
99	
100	
101	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
102	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
103	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
104	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
105	    assert _run() == "ok"
106	
107	
108	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
109	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
110	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
111	        _run()
112	
113	
114	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
115	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
116	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
117	        _run()
118	
119	
120	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
121	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
122	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
123	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
124	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
125	        _run()
126	
127	
128	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
129	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
130	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
131	        _run()
132	
133	
134	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
135	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
136	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
137	        _run()
138	
139	
140	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
141	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
142	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
143	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
144	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
145	        _run()
146	
147	
148	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
149	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
150	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
151	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
152	        _run()
153	
154	
155	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
156	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
157	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
158	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
159	        _run()
160

353	def _high_stakes_auth_present() -> bool:
354	    """Subscription auth available: an exported OAuth token OR a stored CLI credentials file
355	    (the normal state after ``claude login`` without exporting a token)."""
356	    if os.environ.get(OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV):
357	        return True
358	    return (Path.home() / ".claude" / ".credentials.json").is_file()
359	
360	
361	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
362	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
363	    try:
364	        import anyio
365	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
366	            AssistantMessage,
367	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
368	            ResultMessage,
369	            TextBlock,
370	            query,
371	        )
372	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
373	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
374	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
375	        ) from exc
376	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
377	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
378	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
379	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
380	        )
381	
382	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
383	        system_prompt=system,
384	        model=model,
385	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
386	        setting_sources=[],
387	        allowed_tools=[],
388	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
389	    )
390	
391	    parts: list[str] = []
392	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
393	
394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        nonlocal result_error
399	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
400	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
401	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
402	                if rl == "rejected":
403	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
404	                continue
405	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
406	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
407	                continue
408	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
409	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
410	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
411	                        parts.append(block.text)
412	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
413	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
414	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
415	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
416	                ):
417	                    result_error = {
418	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
419	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
420	                    }
421	
422	    try:
423	        anyio.run(_drain)
424	    except Exception as exc:
425	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier call failed: {exc}") from exc
426	
427	    if result_error is not None:
428	        status = result_error.get("status")
429	        detail = result_error.get("detail") or status
430	        if status in (429, 529):
431	            raise RateLimitError(f"high-stakes tier rate window ({status}): {detail}")
432	        if status in (401, 403):
433	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier auth failed ({status}): {detail}")
434	        raise ReasoningError(f"high-stakes tier reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
435	
436	    text = "".join(parts).strip()
437	    if not text:  # empty frontier reply is not a valid result — retry (issue #R5)
438	        raise ReasoningError("high-stakes tier returned an empty reply")
439	    return text
440	
441

453	    if request.tier is StakesTier.BULK:
454	        return cast(

content.text: The claim is accurate about the code. The sandbox settings exist at seam.py:382-389, and the fake `_ClaudeAgentOptions` (test line 53-55) just stores kwargs without any assertion. Let me verify the claim that no drain test asserts these fields, and confirm the env assertion exists/doesn't. Let me also check `scrubbed_env`.

content.text:
Confirmed. The only references to `permission_mode`, `setting_sources`, and `allowed_tools` in the entire repo (outside venv) are the three production lines in seam.py. No test anywhere references or asserts them.

My findings:

1. **Code accuracy**: The reported file/line references are correct. `_high_stakes_complete` constructs `ClaudeAgentOptions` with `permission_mode="dontAsk"`, `setting_sources=[]`, `allowed_tools=[]` at seam.py:382-389. This is the sandbox that prevents the subscription subprocess (running on the production Mac mini) from executing arbitrary tools or reading user/project settings.

2. **Test gap is real and non-discriminating**: The fake `_ClaudeAgentOptions` (test lines 53-55) merely captures kwargs into `__dict__` and is never inspected. No drain test reads `options`. I confirmed via PoC: weakening all three settings to the maximally dangerous values (`permission_mode="bypassPermissions"`, `allowed_tools=["Bash"]`, `setting_sources=["user","project"]`) passes all 73 tests (12 drain + reasoning suite). A regression that removes the sandbox — letting the high-stakes Claude subprocess run arbitrary shell commands and read project/user secrets — would be invisible to CI.

3. **Severity recalibration**: The assessor said HIGH. This is a test-quality / guard-gap finding, not a live exploit — the production code today is correct. But the asset it fails to protect is high-value (arbitrary code execution on the production node, tied to ADR-0003 hardening), and the regression surface is a single-line edit in actively-hardened code. A missing guard on an RCE-prevention sandbox is more than MEDIUM. I concur with HIGH for the security-relevant subset (`permission_mode`/`allowed_tools`/`setting_sources`); the suggested `system_prompt`/`model` threading assertions are lower-value (MEDIUM at best) but reasonable to bundle.

4. Not a duplicate of pass-1: pass-1's `test_scrubbed_env_overwrites_unsafe_and_survives_sdk_merge` guards `scrubbed_env()` (the `env=` field) in isolation, but does NOT guard the three sandbox fields, and does not assert they're actually wired into the constructed `options`.

input.reasoning:
Verified TRUE. The high-stakes seam (src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:382-389) constructs ClaudeAgentOptions with permission_mode="dontAsk", setting_sources=[], allowed_tools=[] — the sandbox that stops the subscription Claude subprocess (running on the production Mac mini) from executing arbitrary tools (e.g. Bash) or reading user/project settings. This is ADR-0003 hardening of an RCE-prevention boundary.

The drain test suite injects a fake _ClaudeAgentOptions (tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py:53-55) that only stores kwargs in __dict__ and is never inspected. No test in the entire repo references permission_mode / setting_sources / allowed_tools — a full-repo grep (excluding .venv) returns only the three production lines. The suite is therefore non-discriminating for the sandbox: a regression weakening it would be invisible to CI.

I PoC-confirmed this. Weakening all three settings to the maximally dangerous values (permission_mode="bypassPermissions", allowed_tools=["Bash"], setting_sources=["user","project"]) and running the suite: 73 passed (12 drain tests + reasoning suite), 0 failures. The exact attack the assessor described (letting the high-stakes subprocess run arbitrary shell on the production node) passes CI cleanly.

Not a pass-1 duplicate: pass-1 added test_scrubbed_env_overwrites_unsafe_and_survives_sdk_merge (tests/test_reasoning.py:71-80) which guards scrubbed_env() (the env= field) in isolation, but neither guards the three sandbox fields nor asserts any field is actually wired into the constructed options.

Severity HIGH for the security-relevant subset (permission_mode/allowed_tools/setting_sources): the guarded asset is arbitrary code execution + secret exposure on the production node, and the regression surface is a one-line edit in actively-hardened code. The assessor's additional suggestion to also assert system_prompt/model threading is lower value (those are correctness, not security) but reasonable to bundle.
input.evidence: PoC: backed up seam.py, replaced permission_mode="dontAsk"->"bypassPermissions", allowed_tools=[]->["Bash"], setting_sources=[]->["user","project"], ran `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py tests/test_reasoning.py -q` => "73 passed in 0.26s" (zero failures despite the sandbox being fully disabled), then restored the file. Repo-wide grep `grep -rn "permission_mode|setting_sources|allowed_tools" --include="*.py" .` (excl .venv) returns only src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:385-387 — no test asserts them. Fake options class: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py:53-55 (`self.__dict__.update(kw)`), captured `scripted`/`options` never asserted. Suggested fix: capture the constructed ClaudeAgentOptions in the fake (or via a monkeypatched spy) and assert permission_mode=="dontAsk", allowed_tools==[], setting_sources==[] (and system_prompt/model threaded).

content.text: Verified TRUE. The pass-2 finding holds: the high-stakes subprocess sandbox (`permission_mode`/`setting_sources`/`allowed_tools` at seam.py:382-389) is correct in production but completely unguarded by tests. I PoC-confirmed non-discrimination — fully weakening the sandbox to an arbitrary-RCE configuration passes all 73 tests. It's not a pass-1 duplicate (pass-1 only guards `scrubbed_env()`/the `env=` field). Severity HIGH for the security-critical subset; the bundled `system_prompt`/`model` threading assertion is lower value but fine to include.
